title: Ceropegia semota
taxonomy: Ceropegia semota is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander family. The genus Ceropegia is a type of flowering succulent that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It belongs to the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. Ceropegia semota is further classified within the subfamily Rauvoloideae, which is a group of flowering plants that are known for their unique, twisted flowers. The exact classification of Ceropegia semota may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research.

intro: Ceropegia semota, also known as the 'witch's thimble,' is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Ceropegia semota has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, particularly semota alkaloids, which have been identified as the primary active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Ceropegia semota is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential uses.
